metaDescription: Mohtaoua knowledge center: marketing, development, AI, automation, branding and digital transformation.
title: <highlight>digital knowledge center</highlight>
description: Marketing, development, AI, automation, branding and digital transformation.

author.name: Team Mohtaoua
author.bio: Experts in marketing, technology, design and automation for companies in all sectors in Morocco.

generer-leads-centre-formation-maroc.title: How to generate qualified leads for your business in Morocco
generer-leads-centre-formation-maroc.excerpt: Complete acquisition strategy: Meta Ads, Google Ads, landing pages and CRM for SMEs, startups and large accounts.

sections.heading: Why lead generation is critical
paragraphs: Businesses in Morocco face increased competition and shorter sales cycles. Without a pipeline of qualified prospects, sales goals are missed.
Lead generation is not an option: it is the most predictable growth engine for a business.

sections.heading: Channels that work in 2026
paragraphs: Meta Ads remains the fastest channel to create demand. Google Ads captures high-intent searches (“your offer + city”). SEO and content fuel long-term credibility.
bullets: Meta Ads: volume and speed
Google Ads: high purchase intent
Dedicated landing pages: conversion
CRM + automation: monitoring and closing

sections.heading: What budget should you plan?
paragraphs: For a center in the digital launch phase, count on 8,000 to 25,000 MAD/month of media budget, excluding agency fees. The optimal budget depends on the offer price and the target acquisition cost.

sections.heading: KPIs to follow
paragraphs: Don’t stop at the number of leads. Monitor the cost per lead (CPL), the contact rate, the appointment rate and above all the customer acquisition cost (CAC) per offer.

faqs.question: How many leads do you need to fill a session?
faqs.answer: Generally 3 to 5 qualified leads per conversion depending on your conversion rate. For 20 places, aim for 60 to 100 qualified leads in the cycle.

faqs.question: Do you have to be on all channels?
faqs.answer: No. Start with 1-2 mastered channels (often Meta + Google) before expanding.

overview.what: Complete guide to generating qualified leads via digital in Morocco, whatever your sector.
overview.who: Executives, marketing directors and founders of SMEs, startups and growing companies.
takeaways: Lead generation is the #1 driver of growth

meta-ads-vs-google-ads-formation.title: Meta Ads vs Google Ads: which channel for your business in Morocco?
meta-ads-vs-google-ads-formation.excerpt: Objective comparison for e-commerce, services, health, real estate and B2B.

sections.heading: Meta Ads: creating demand
paragraphs: Meta (Facebook & Instagram) excels at reaching audiences who are not yet actively looking for an offer. Ideal for launches, promotions and awareness.

sections.heading: Google Ads: capturing demand
paragraphs: Google intercepts searches with commercial intent. Perfect for sought-after Offers (languages, IT, health) with often higher ROAS in the short term.

sections.heading: Our recommendation
paragraphs: For most businesses, the combination of Meta (volume) + Google (intent) over 3 months offers the best balance of cost and quality.

faqs.question: Which channel has the lowest CPL?
faqs.answer: Variable depending on the offer. Meta is often cheaper in volume; Google may be more qualified.

checklist-landing-page-formation.title: Checklist: landing page that converts (2026)
checklist-landing-page-formation.excerpt: 10 essential elements to transform advertising traffic into leads and sales.

sections.heading: 1. A clear and profit-oriented hero
paragraphs: Title = promised result. Subtitle = for whom. CTA visible without scrolling on mobile.

sections.heading: 2. Social proof
paragraphs: Testimonials, registration figures, certifications, partner logos.

sections.heading: 3. Short form
paragraphs: Name, telephone, email are enough as a first step. Then qualify by automation.

faqs.question: Is one page required per offer?
faqs.answer: Yes, to align the advertising message and maximize the conversion rate.

whatsapp-convertir-leads-formation-maroc.title: WhatsApp: convert your leads into customers in Morocco
whatsapp-convertir-leads-formation-maroc.excerpt: Scripts, automation and CRM to transform your WhatsApp leads into sales.

overview.what: Complete method for converting WhatsApp leads into customers, across all sectors.
overview.who: Salespeople, marketing directors and executives who receive leads via WhatsApp.
takeaways: WhatsApp is the number 1 channel in Morocco

sections.heading: Introduction: the missing link in your acquisition
paragraphs: You invest in Meta Ads, Google Ads or SEO. Your landing page generates leads. Then… silence. Prospects no longer respond, your team follows up too late, and conversions don't happen.

In Morocco, this scenario is almost systematic among companies which treat WhatsApp as “personal messaging” rather than a structured sales channel. However, WhatsApp is the preferred point of contact for the majority of future prospects after a request for information.
This article details the method that we deploy at Mohtaoua for our clients: from the first automatic response to conversion confirmation, including qualification and CRM integration.

sections.heading: The problem: you generate leads that you don't convert
paragraphs: Most businesses measure success by CPL (cost per lead) and stop there. But a 15 MAD lead that never responds costs infinitely more than a 40 MAD lead converted into conversion.

Recurring causes: response time greater than 2 hours, generic copy-pasted messages, lack of qualification (budget, availability, level), reminders forgotten in personal conversations, and no management visibility on the pipeline.
bullets: Leads processed on the salesperson's personal WhatsApp
No consistent qualification script
Manual reminders forgotten after 48 hours
No link between advertising campaign and final conversion

sections.heading: The solution: a structured and measurable WhatsApp tunnel
paragraphs: Conversion begins the moment the prospect clicks “Send” or leaves their number. Every minute counts: a response in less than 5 minutes increases the chances of getting an appointment by 3.
The solution is not to send more messages, but to build a clear path: immediate welcome → qualification in 3 questions → niche proposal → reminder D+1/D+3 → commercial contact if hot lead.
This funnel relies on WhatsApp Business (or API for high volumes), a connected CRM, and automation scenarios for standard follow-ups — while maintaining human intervention on qualified leads.

sections.heading: Why WhatsApp dominates in Morocco for your activity
paragraphs: In Morocco, WhatsApp goes far beyond email and often telephone for the first interaction after an advertisement. Prospects want a quick response, in Darija, French or Arabic, with the possibility of sending documents (CV, diplomas) in one click.

The companies that perform best have understood that WhatsApp is not an “extra” channel: it is often the main closing channel, especially for high-ticket offers, languages, and bootcamps whose average ticket justifies a human exchange.

sections.heading: Step by step: set up your WhatsApp tunnel
paragraphs: Here are the six steps we recommend to any center that is starting or wants to professionalize its WhatsApp flow.
bullets: Step 1 — Centralize: a dedicated WhatsApp Business number (not the manager’s personal)
Step 2 — Auto-welcome: welcome message in less than 60 seconds with promise to call back
Step 3 — Qualification: 3 questions (target offer, city, conversion deadline)
Step 4 — Make an appointment: Calendly link or slots offered directly in the chat
Step 5 — Reminders D+1, D+3, D+7 if no response (short messages, not spam)
Step 6 — CRM closure: status “registered”, “lost”, “to be contacted again” with reason

sections.heading: Message scripts that convert
paragraphs: A good WhatsApp script is short, personalized and oriented towards a single action. Avoid blocks of text copied from your commercial brochure.

Example of greeting: “Hello [First name], thank you for your interest in [Training] at [Centre]. I am [First name advisor]. Do you have 2 minutes for me to ask you 3 quick questions and direct you to the next session? »

Example of follow-up D+3: “Hello [First name], I would like to get back to you regarding [Training]. The next session starts on [Date] — there are [X] places left. Would you like a 10 minute call to discuss this? »

sections.heading: Automation: what to automate (and what to keep human)
paragraphs: Automate the welcome, standard reminders, appointment reminders and post-visit messages. Keep the negotiation, price objections, and conclusion of conversion human.

Tools like Make, Zapier, HubSpot or local solutions allow you to connect your Meta/Google → CRM → WhatsApp form. The objective: zero leads without a first response, even outside of business hours (auto message + promise to call back the next day at 9 a.m.).

sections.heading: Integrate WhatsApp into your CRM
paragraphs: Without CRM, it is impossible to know which lead comes from which campaign, which salesperson followed up, or what your real lead → conversion rate is. This is the blind spot of most businesses in Morocco.

A well-configured CRM displays: source (Meta, Google, organic), lead date, WhatsApp history, status (new, contacted, appointment, registered, lost), and responsible. You can then calculate an actual CAC per channel and optimize your advertising budgets accordingly.

sections.heading: Best practices from centers that convert best
paragraphs: The centers with the best WhatsApp conversion rates share common habits, regardless of their sector (languages, IT, health, management).
bullets: Median response within 5 minutes in business hours
A single WhatsApp pro number displayed everywhere (site, ads, signature)
Scripts validated but adapted to the tone of each advisor
Systematic reminder on D+1, D+3, D+7 then archiving
Appointment confirmed by message the day before with video link or address
Weekly management feedback: leads, appointments, conversions, CAC

sections.heading: Common mistakes to avoid
paragraphs: These errors cost a lot of money in lost leads. We see them every week during our free audits.
bullets: Reply 24 hours later “Sorry for the delay” — the prospect is already with the competitor
Send the PDF brochure without asking a qualifying question
Use personal WhatsApp without backup or transfer
Spam 5 messages in a row if no response
Do not track the source of the lead in the CRM
Confusing “seen” and “processed” — a message read without a response = cold lead

sections.heading: KPIs to follow each week
paragraphs: Measure what matters for conversion, not just the volume of messages sent.
bullets: Median first response time
Contact rate (leads having responded at least once)
Rate of appointments made / leads contacted
Conversion rate / appointments made
CAC by channel (Meta, Google, organic)
Reason for loss (price, timing, competition, no response)

sections.heading: Conclusion: WhatsApp is your closing room
paragraphs: Generating leads without structuring WhatsApp is like filling a leaky tank. In Morocco, conversion is played out in conversation — speed, clarity, follow-up.
Start with a professional number, a welcome script, three automatic follow-ups and a minimal CRM. Then refine with automation and the increase in the skills of your advisors.
Need an audit of your current tunnel? Our team analyzes your lead → WhatsApp → conversion flow and provides you with a concrete action plan within 24 hours.

faqs.question: Should you use WhatsApp Business or the WhatsApp API?
faqs.answer: WhatsApp Business is sufficient for up to ~50 leads/week with 2-3 advisors. Beyond that, or to connect to the CRM and automate on a large scale, the API via a partner (Twilio, 360dialog, etc.) becomes necessary.

faqs.question: What response time should you aim for?
faqs.answer: Less than 5 minutes during business hours. Outside of hours, an immediate auto message + promised reminder the next morning maintains the commitment.

faqs.question: How many follow-ups before abandoning a lead?
faqs.answer: Three restarts spaced apart (D+1, D+3, D+7) is the standard. Beyond that, archive in “to contact again” for a monthly reactivation campaign.

faqs.question: Can we send messages in Darija?
faqs.answer: Yes, and it is often more effective for the first contact. Adapt the tone to your audience: Darija, French or Arabic depending on the profile.

faqs.question: How to link WhatsApp to Meta Ads?
faqs.answer: Use Lead Ads forms with a telephone field, or a WhatsApp button on the landing. Connect the flow to your CRM via Zapier/Make to centralize leads.

faqs.question: What lead → conversion rate is realistic?
faqs.answer: Between 8% and 25% depending on price, seasonality and quality of monitoring. With an optimized WhatsApp funnel, +5 conversion points is an achievable goal in 60 days.

faqs.question: Doesn’t automation make you “robot”?
faqs.answer: Welcome and reminders can be automated while remaining personalized (first name, offer). The closing phase remains human.

faqs.question: Can Mohtaoua set up this tunnel?
faqs.answer: Yes. We integrate CRM, automation and WhatsApp scripts into our Performance and Premium packs. Request a free audit to assess your current situation.

budget-marketing-centre-formation-maroc.title: Digital marketing budget: how much to invest in Morocco?
budget-marketing-centre-formation-maroc.excerpt: Realistic ranges for SMEs and businesses: ads, content, web and tools.

overview.what: Quantified guide to define the marketing budget of a company in Morocco: media, production, tools and agency services.
overview.who: Center directors, marketing managers or managers who launch or optimize their digital acquisition.
benefits: 2026 budget ranges adapted to the Moroccan market
Optimal distribution Meta Ads vs Google Ads
takeaways: Allow 8,000 to 25,000 MAD/month of media to start an offer
CPL alone is not enough: track cost per conversion
70/30 Meta/Google is a good starting point, to be adjusted by data

sections.heading: Introduction: why the marketing budget is scary (wrongly)
paragraphs: “How much should I spend on advertising? » is the first question during an audit. The right question is: how much does a conversion cost, and how many do you need to achieve your business goals?

sections.heading: The 4 budget items to distinguish
paragraphs: Confusing media budget and total budget is mistake #1. Here is the breakdown that we use at Mohtaoua.
bullets: Media budget: Meta Ads, Google Ads, TikTok — paid to the platforms
Production: creative videos, photos, landing pages
Tools: CRM, automation, tracking, landing hosting
Agency service: strategy, management, optimization, reporting

sections.heading: Realistic ranges for Morocco in 2026
paragraphs: These figures concern the monthly media budget, excluding agency fees, for a center with 1 to 3 active offers.
bullets: Launch / test: 8,000 – 12,000 MAD/month
Growth: 15,000 – 25,000 MAD/month
Multi-city or multi-offer scale: 30,000 – 80,000 MAD/month
Typical pro CPL: 25 – 80 MAD depending on niche and city

sections.heading: The calculation method: from conversion to budget
paragraphs: Start from your business objective, not the available budget.
Example: 20 conversions × 3 months = 60 conversions. Lead → conversion rate: 15%. You need ~400 qualified leads. Target CPL: 40 MAD → media budget ≈ 16,000 MAD/month over 3 months.

sections.heading: How to split between Meta Ads and Google Ads
paragraphs: The distribution depends on your maturity and the existing demand for your offer.
bullets: Launch phase: 70% Meta / 30% Google — create demand
Mature offer (languages, IT): 50/50 or 40% Meta / 60% Google
Monthly reallocation according to CPL and conversion rate per channel
Always reserve 10–15% of the budget for creative testing

sections.heading: Costly budgeting mistakes
paragraphs: These pitfalls appear in 8 out of 10 audits.
bullets: Stop campaigns before 14 days of algorithmic learning
Compare CPL without looking at lead quality
Forgetting the cost of creative production in the overall calculation
Increase the budget by 300% at once (reset learning phase)
Do not track conversions back to the advertising source

sections.heading: Seasonality of conversions in Morocco
paragraphs: Anticipating peaks (September, January, pre-summer launch) allows ROAS to be optimized. Set up the budget 4–6 weeks before the conversions open date, not the day before.

sections.heading: Conclusion: invest methodically, not by feeling
paragraphs: A marketing budget is not an expense: it is a measurable conversion lever. Set your target CAC, calculate the volume of leads needed, test over 90 days, then scale what converts.
Our team carries out a free budget audit: analysis of your offer, CPL/CAC simulation and Meta/Google distribution recommendation.

faqs.question: What is the minimum budget for testing Meta Ads?
faqs.answer: 8,000 MAD/month of media in an area (e.g. Casablanca) and an offer, for 60 to 90 days, to obtain usable data.

faqs.question: Should agency fees be included in the media budget?
faqs.answer: No. The media budget goes to the platforms. Agency fees are a separate item, often 15–25% of the media or monthly fee.

faqs.question: Which CPL is considered good in Morocco?
faqs.answer: Between 25 and 55 MAD for a well-targeted professional offer. Beyond 80 MAD, check targeting, creative and landing page.

faqs.question: Can we start with less than 8,000 MAD/month?
faqs.answer: Possible for a very localized niche, but volumes will be low and Meta/Google learning will be slow. Better to focus on one offer and one city.

faqs.question: How does Mohtaoua structure its price packs?
faqs.answer: Our Start, Performance and Premium packs include management, creation and reporting. The media budget remains your responsibility and is managed with you. See /pricing.

faqs.question: When to increase the budget?
faqs.answer: When the CPL is stable, the lead rate → RDV > 30% and the CAC remains below your target margin for 2 consecutive weeks.

campagne-inscriptions-rentree-formation-.title: Launch a successful digital acquisition campaign
campagne-inscriptions-rentree-formation-.excerpt: Calendar, creative, budget and optimization for your product or seasonal launches.

overview.what: Operational plan to launch and manage a seasonal digital acquisition campaign in Morocco (product launch, promotion, peak demand).
overview.who: Marketing directors and managers who are preparing for a commercial peak (September, January, Ramadan, sales) and must achieve their objectives on time.
benefits: Meta Ads and Google Ads structure adapted to seasonality
Offer scripts, landing pages and WhatsApp reminders ready to deploy
takeaways: Anticipate 6 to 8 weeks before the peak
Test creatives and offers in advance
Measure CAC, not just lead volume

sections.heading: Introduction: a seasonal launch is prepared 6 to 8 weeks before the peak
paragraphs: Every year, companies contact us too late: commercial objectives far from being achieved, budget to be burned urgently. A successful seasonal launch is being prepared from July for a peak in September — or earlier depending on your sector (sales, Ramadan, back-to-school).
In Morocco, digital demand is exploding over predictable windows. Those who structure their Meta, Google and WhatsApp campaigns convert with a controlled CAC. Others pay 2x more per lead.

sections.heading: The ideal calendar: July → September
paragraphs: Here is the schedule that we apply at Mohtaoua for our business clients in Morocco.
bullets: July (H1–H2): offer audit, dedicated landing page, video creatives, tracking conversions
July (S3–S4): launch of Meta + Google test campaigns, moderate budget (60% of peak)
August (S1–S2): scale of winning audiences, +30% budget, launch of early-bird offer
August (W3–S4): intensification, visitor retargeting, WhatsApp campaigns on lukewarm leads
September (S1): “last places” emergency, aggressive retargeting, advisor reminders
September (S2+): acquisition switch → nurturing on waiting list next wave

sections.heading: Structuring the seasonal offer that converts
paragraphs: A seasonal campaign does not sell a generic offer: it sells an opportunity limited in time with a clear benefit (promo, places, certification, delivery before date).
Messages that work combine gentle urgency, social proof and friction reduction (installment payments, guarantees, bonuses).
bullets: Early bird: -10 to -15% or free handling fees until a fixed date
Real emergency: stock or limited places (show the figure, not “soon full”)
Social proof: video testimonials from alumni, insertion rate, partner company logos
Unique CTA: “Take advantage of the offer” rather than “Learn more”

sections.heading: Meta Ads: seasonal campaign structure
paragraphs: On Meta, the seasonal launch is a receptive cold audience period. Structure into 3 distinct campaigns to keep budget control.
bullets: Campaign 1 — Prospecting: Advantage+ or CBO, 3–5 creatives (15–30 s video, testimonials carousel)
Campaign 2 — Retargeting: visitors landing 7/14/30 days, Facebook/Instagram page engagement
Campaign 3 — Hot leads: lookalike 1–3% on N-1 customers or converted customers
Targeting: 22–45 years old, business interests + target professions, geo city + 25 km
Budget: start at 150–250 MAD/day per offer, increase to 400–600 MAD/day in peak August

sections.heading: Google Ads: capturing search intent
paragraphs: In seasonal launch, Google Ads becomes strategic: prospects type “offer [business] Casablanca”, “conversion seasonal launch [domain]”. Don't leave those clicks to your competitors.
bullets: Search campaigns: transactional keywords (conversion, price, seasonal launch, September session)
Performance Max campaigns: with seasonal launch assets + feed if multi-offer catalog
RSA announcements: mention start date, early-bird, city, certification
Extensions: additional links (program, testimonials, contact), call, location
Auctions: Target CPA once 30+ conversions/month, otherwise Maximize Conversions capped

sections.heading: Landing page, WhatsApp and reminders
paragraphs: Seasonal traffic is expensive. Each unconverted visitor must enter a follow-up funnel.
Your landing page must display the deadline, the stock or remaining places, the 5-point offer and a short form (name, telephone, offer).
bullets: WhatsApp: response < 5 min, welcome script with offer date + deposit payment link
Follow-up D+1, D+3, D+7 on unconverted leads (automation or CRM)
Retargeting: “Your limited offer is waiting for you” ad with countdown
Tracking: Meta pixel + Google conversions + CRM conversion event

sections.heading: 5 mistakes that cause you to miss the seasonal launch
paragraphs: These errors recur every year in our pre-launch audits.
bullets: Wait until September to launch campaigns (CPL ×2, maximum commercial pressure)
A single creative for 6 weeks (audience fatigue, drop in CTR)
Generic landing page without mention of “September launch”
No retargeting: 97% of visitors leave without converting
Undersized sales team in August (unprocessed leads = wasted budget)

sections.heading: Conclusion: anticipate, structure, scale
paragraphs: A seasonal peak is expected. Plan in advance, test in quiet times, intensify at the right time.
Need a turnkey plan? Our team structures your Meta/Google campaigns, optimizes your landing and configures your WhatsApp reminders.

faqs.question: When to launch seasonal campaigns in September?
faqs.answer: Ideally mid-July for testing, scale mid-August. Minimum: early August with aggressive budget and early-bird offer.

faqs.question: What media budget for a seasonal campaign?
faqs.answer: 15,000 to 30,000 MAD/month in July–August for a main offer in a single city. Multiply by the number of active offers.

faqs.question: Meta Ads or Google Ads as a priority for the seasonal launch?
faqs.answer: Meta to create demand and reach the undecided. Google to capture active search. Start 65/35 Meta/Google, adjust according to CPL and conversion rate.

faqs.question: Is there a need for a dedicated seasonal launch landing page?
faqs.answer: Yes. A specific page with date, stock/places, early-bird and testimonials converts 2 to 3x better than a generic page.

faqs.question: How to manage leads that don't convert before September?
faqs.answer: Automated reminders, retargeting, then switching to the waiting list for the October or January session. Don't throw away leads: the seasonal launch lasts 6 weeks.

faqs.question: Can Mohtaoua manage our seasonal campaign?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer a seasonal launch pack: strategy, creation, Meta/Google management, landing page and WhatsApp automation. Request a free audit on /contact.

transformation-digitale-pme-maroc.title: digital transformation of SMEs in Morocco: where to start in 2026
transformation-digitale-pme-maroc.excerpt: Pragmatic roadmap: website, marketing, CRM, automation and AI to accelerate the growth of your SME.

overview.what: Strategic guide to launching the digital transformation of an SME in Morocco without dispersing yourself.
overview.who: Managers of SMEs, startups and ETIs who want to structure their digital growth.
takeaways: Start with the basics: site + tracking
AI amplifies, it does not replace strategy

sections.heading: Why digital transformation is no longer optional
paragraphs: In Morocco, SMEs that structure their digital presence grow 2 to 3 times faster than those that rely solely on word of mouth. Your customers compare online, contact via WhatsApp and expect an immediate response.
Digital transformation is not “redoing a site”: it is aligning marketing, technology, data and customer experience to generate more revenue with less friction.

sections.heading: Step 1 — The Foundations (weeks 1-4)
paragraphs: Before investing in advertising, make sure the foundations are solid.
bullets: Professional, fast and mobile-first website
Tracking conversions (Meta Pixel, GA4, events)
WhatsApp Business or dedicated professional number
Updated Google Business Profile

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Acquisition (months 2-3)
paragraphs: Once the foundations are laid, activate the acquisition channels adapted to your sector: Meta for created demand, Google for search intent, SEO for the long term.
bullets: Landing page by main offer
Meta or Google campaigns with test budget 8-15K MAD/month
Creatives adapted to your Moroccan audience

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Conversion and CRM (months 3-4)
paragraphs: Leads without structured follow-up get lost. Implement a lightweight CRM (HubSpot, Pipedrive) and connect it to your forms and WhatsApp.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Automation and AI (months 4-6)
paragraphs: Automate reminders, appointment reminders and repetitive tasks. Explore AI for customer support and lead qualification — always with human supervision.

sections.heading: Realistic budget for an SME
paragraphs: Allow 15,000 to 50,000 MAD/month depending on the ambition: agency fees + media budget + tools (CRM, automation). A free audit allows precise calibration.

faqs.question: Where to start if the budget is limited?
faqs.answer: Professional website + Google Business Profile + WhatsApp Business structured. Then a Meta or Google test campaign.

faqs.question: Should we internalize everything?
faqs.answer: No. A 360° agency like Mohtaoua covers marketing, web and tech — you keep the business focus.

faqs.question: How long does it take to see results?
faqs.answer: First leads in 2-4 weeks with advertising. ROI stabilized over 60-90 days with continuous optimization.

retargeting-leads-formation-maroc.title: Meta & Google retargeting: recover prospects who have not yet registered
retargeting-leads-formation-maroc.excerpt: Audiences, time windows, budgets and creative to turn 20–35% of your visitors and lukewarm leads into registrations.

overview.what: Practical guide to structuring your Meta Ads and Google Ads retargeting campaigns and recovering training prospects who did not convert on the first pass.
overview.who: Marketing managers and training center directors who generate leads but struggle to convert them into registrations.
benefits: Meta and Google audience structure ready to deploy
Distribution of prospecting vs retargeting budget
Creative scripts and emergency messages that convert
takeaways: Dedicate 20–30% of media budget to retargeting once 500+ visitors/month
Segment by intention: landing visitors, form leads, engaged social networks
A lukewarm lead converts 3 to 5x better in retargeting than a cold audience

sections.heading: Introduction: why retargeting is your best ROI
paragraphs: 97% of visitors leave your landing page without leaving contact details. Among the 3% who become leads, only 10 to 20% register on first contact. The rest is not lost: it is lukewarm.

Retargeting allows you to redisplay your ads to people who already know your center. In Morocco, for professional training, the cost per registration for retargeting is often 40 to 60% lower than cold traffic — provided you structure your audiences correctly.

sections.heading: Understanding the funnel: who to retarget and when
paragraphs: Not all hot audiences are created equal. Prioritize by level of intention.
bullets: Level 1 — Visitors landing 7 days: saw your offer, not converted (maximum priority)
Level 2 — Leads form not converted 14 days: have left their contact details
Level 3 — 30-day social engagement: 50%+ video, Messenger click, comment
Level 4 — Visitors 30–90 days: large audience, gentle reminder messages
Systematically exclude: registrants, customers, converted leads < 30 days

sections.heading: Meta Ads: audiences and campaign structure
paragraphs: On Meta, create a dedicated retargeting campaign separate from prospecting. CBO (Campaign Budget Optimization) works well with 2 to 4 ad sets per intent level.
bullets: Custom Audience: site visitors 7/14/30 days (Meta pixel + ViewContent event)
Custom Audience: leads (Lead event or uploaded, hashed CRM list)
Audience Engagement: Facebook/Instagram page visitors, interactions 30 days
Exclusion: buyers/registrants + converted leads (CompleteRegistration event)
Placements: Advantage+ placements, unless vertical videos → Stories/Reels only
Budget: 20–30% of the total budget, minimum 80–120 MAD/day per offer

sections.heading: Google Ads: Search and Display remarketing
paragraphs: Google offers two levers: Display/YouTube remarketing for visual recall, and Search remarketing audiences to capture recall searches (“training X price”, “center Y reviews”).
bullets: Site remarketing list: Google Ads tag + consent mode (GDPR/cookies)
Responsive Display campaign: back-to-school banners, testimonials, early-bird
RLSA (Remarketing Lists for Search Ads): +20 to +50% bids on hot audiences
Performance Max: remarketing audience signal in dedicated asset group
Window: 7 days for emergency, 30 days for recall, 90 days max training
Exclusion: converters 30 days in all acquisition campaigns

sections.heading: Creatives and messages that convert into retargeting
paragraphs: Don't reuse your creative prospecting. Retargeting speaks to someone who knows you: be direct, personal, action-oriented.
bullets: Emergency angle: “There are X places left for the [month] session”
Angle reminder: “You consulted [training] — a question? »
Social proof angle: 15 sec video testimony from a former member of the same training
Offer angle: early-bird reminder with visible deadline
Format: short video > static image; carousel to compare sessions
CTA: “Reserve my seat” / “Talk to an advisor” — not “Discover”

sections.heading: Budget and distribution of prospecting/retargeting
paragraphs: The rule we apply at Mohtaoua: as long as you have less than 300 unique visitors/month on the landing, retargeting lacks volume. Beyond 500, activate it systematically.
bullets: Test phase (< 500 visitors/month): 90% prospecting / 10% retargeting
Growth phase (500–2,000 visitors): 75% prospecting / 25% retargeting
Scale phase (> 2,000 visitors): 65–70% prospecting / 30–35% retargeting
Seasonal peak (back to school): increase retargeting to 40% in September
Meta frequency: monitor, cap at 3–4 impressions/day per person
Retargeting KPI: cost per registration, not CPL (leads already exist)

sections.heading: Essential tracking before launching
paragraphs: Without proper tracking, your audiences will be empty or polluted. Check these prerequisites.
bullets: Pixel Meta installed + Lead and CompleteRegistration events configured
Google tag + imported conversions (form, phone click, thank you page)
Consent mode v2 activated (compliant cookies banner)
Consistent UTMs across all campaigns for CRM attribution
Exclusion of internal teams and bots (IP filter if necessary)

sections.heading: Common retargeting errors
paragraphs: These errors explain why many centers abandon retargeting after 2 weeks.
bullets: Mix prospecting and retargeting in the same CBO campaign
Retarget all site traffic (blog, contact) instead of landing training
Frequency too high → fatigue, unsubscriptions, CPL rising
No exclusion of registrants → waste and bad experience
Creative identical to prospecting → no personalization
Stop retargeting as soon as a lead is created (the lead is not a registration)

sections.heading: Conclusion: retargeting completes, does not replace
paragraphs: Retargeting does not create demand: it recovers it. Used in addition to your prospecting campaigns and your WhatsApp reminders, it can represent 20 to 35% of your monthly registrations at a reduced cost.
Our team configures your Meta/Google audiences, your CRM exclusions and your retargeting creatives in less than a week. Request a free audit on /contact.

faqs.question: How many visitors should you activate retargeting?
faqs.answer: From 500 unique visitors/month to your training landing. Below that, the audiences are too small and the CPM remains high.

faqs.question: What is the minimum daily budget for Meta retargeting?
faqs.answer: 80 to 120 MAD/day per offer. Below 50 MAD/day, the diffusion is too irregular to exit the learning phase.

faqs.question: Should we retarget leads who filled out the form?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is the most profitable audience. Combine Meta/Google retargeting with WhatsApp reminders D+1, D+3, D+7 to maximize conversion.

faqs.question: Meta Ads or Google Ads as a priority for retargeting?
faqs.answer: Meta first: more volume, engaging video formats, often lower cost. Google in addition for RLSA and Display reminder on brand searches.

faqs.question: How to avoid over-frequency of ads?
faqs.answer: Cap the frequency at 3–4/day on Meta, use short windows (7–14 days) for urgency, and run 2–3 creatives per week.

faqs.question: Can Mohtaoua configure our retargeting campaigns?
faqs.answer: Yes. We install tracking, create audiences, produce creative and manage the prospecting/retargeting budget. See our packs on /pricing or request an audit on /contact.
